---
title: "About"
subtitle: "Building an evidence-based, precautionary approach to the management of jumbo flying squid (*Dosidicus gigas*) within SPRFMO."
format: html
---

## The project

The jumbo flying squid (*Dosidicus gigas*) fishery is managed under the
South Pacific Regional Fisheries Management Organisation (SPRFMO), whose
Scientific Committee currently has to weigh several competing population
models — developed independently in Peru, Chile, and China — that
disagree on the stock's status and on the effort levels it can sustain.

This project exists to help the Scientific Committee navigate that
disagreement rather than resolve it prematurely. Rather than asking which
single model is "correct," the work develops complementary lines of
analysis — weighting projections against data and expert judgement, testing
model robustness against alternative harvest control rules, and refining
the underlying assessment directly — so that Committee members have more, and
better-organised, information to draw on when setting precautionary
management measures.

The immediate goal is to bring at least one of these lines to a stage where
it can be presented as a concrete implementation scheme at the **14th
Meeting of the SPRFMO Scientific Committee (SC14, September 2026)**. This
site tracks that progress: the working paper is available under
[Models](models.qmd) and [Risk](docs/modelos.qmd), and presentation material
under [Slides](slides.qmd).

## Who's involved

The work is carried out by Drs **Juan-Carlos Quiroz**
([ORCID](https://orcid.org/0000-0002-2831-7689){target="_blank"}) and
**Renato Gozzer**
([ORCID](https://orcid.org/0000-0002-1156-4552){target="_blank"}).

It is funded by the [Innovations for Ocean Action Foundation (I4OA)](https://ocean-action.org){target="_blank"},
with support from [Facile Visual](https://www.facilevisual.com){target="_blank"}
and [CALAMASUR](https://www.calamasur.com){target="_blank"}.

The associated working paper is archived and citable at
[10.5281/zenodo.21361536](https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.21361536){target="_blank"}.
